concept_seed.one_liner: A dynamic independent podcast network website with bold dark-mode UI, neon gradient accents, animated waveform motifs, show landing pages, episode archives with transcripts, host bios, advertiser/partnership page, live tour dates, a member-supported club tier, and a global persistent audio player.

accepted_concept.domain: Independent podcast network / late-night talk collective
accepted_concept.site_goal: Make Frequency Room feel like a high-design listening lounge for night owls — a glossy magenta-lit broadcast booth where each show feels like its own intimate channel, the episode archive reads like a deep crate of liner-noted records, and the Inner Circle membership tier feels like getting handed an after-hours key.
audience: Night-owl listeners who consume long-form audio
Brand sponsors seeking premium host-read placements
Live taping ticket buyers and superfans
accepted_concept.description: Frequency Room leans editorial and atmospheric where Static Hum leans aggressive. The aesthetic is a late-night FM control room — near-black canvas, hairline lime dividers, magenta neon used sparingly as glow accents around hero portraits and CTA buttons, and a recurring concentric-ring waveform motif (think a vinyl groove crossed with a tuner dial) that animates subtly as audio plays. Typography pairs a tall geometric grotesk for display with a slab-leaning text serif for episode body copy and transcripts, giving the archive a 'liner notes' feeling. Density is medium-high: long scrolling pages with rich metadata, color-coded show channels (each show gets its own accent hue derived from the magenta-lime gradient range), and a global player that minimizes into a vertical waveform rail along the right edge on desktop. Imagery favors moody studio photography of hosts in low magenta light.
accepted_concept.motif: Concentric tuner-dial waveform rings that pulse with playback, paired with hairline lime dividers and soft magenta neon halos around portraits and primary CTAs

pages.layout_pattern: Editorial homepage — full-bleed hero with a single 'Tonight on Frequency Room' featured episode (large host portrait under magenta neon, concentric waveform behind), then a horizontal channel selector strip (one chip per show, each with its own accent hue), an editor's-picks two-column 'Crate' block, a member tier teaser card, and a tour dates strip
sections: Tonight on Frequency Room hero (featured episode, neon-lit portrait)
Channel selector strip (one chip per show, accent-hued)
Upcoming live broadcasts / tour dates strip
Network masthead with founding date and manifesto link

pages.layout_pattern: Roster as a 'channel guide' — vertical list of shows styled like an over-the-air TV grid; each row spans the page with channel number, show logotype, hosts, cadence, current-season episode count, latest-episode play button, and a small animated waveform ribbon
sections: Filter by genre, cadence, season state (active / archived / hiatus)
Sort by recently updated, alphabetical, channel number

pages.layout_pattern: Per-show channel page — sticky left rail with show identity (logo, channel number, hosts, subscribe chips, accent color stripe), main column holds a featured-season hero, season selector tabs, and an episode list rendered as 'liner notes' rows with episode number, title, runtime, guest, one-line tease, and inline play/transcript controls
sections: Sticky show identity rail with subscribe chips
Featured-season hero with art and synopsis
Liner-notes episode list (episode #, title, guests, runtime, tease)

pages.layout_pattern: Cross-network episode archive — top bar with full-text transcript search, faceted filters (show, host, guest, year, topic tag, length bucket), and results rendered as dense scannable rows with highlighted transcript snippets where the search term appears
sections: Full-text transcript search bar with autosuggest
Faceted filter rail (show, host, guest, year, topic, length)
Search-result rows with highlighted transcript snippets
Saved searches for Inner Circle members

pages.layout_pattern: Liner-notes episode page — masthead with episode artwork (concentric waveform rings centered), three-column metadata block (hosts / guests / chapters), full transcript rendered as long-form readable prose with speaker drop-caps, sidebar with 'Mentioned' links and 'If you liked this' recommendations
sections: Inline player with waveform scrubber and chapter jump
Long-form transcript with speaker drop-caps and timestamp gutter
If you liked this — recommendation list

pages.layout_pattern: Host directory styled like a 'staff roll' — large portraits in low magenta light arranged in a 4-column grid with handwritten-style signature beneath each name; clicking a portrait expands a full-bleed feature with bio, career timeline, shows hosted, and a curated 'self-selected episodes' list
sections: Staff-roll portrait grid (4-column, low magenta light)
Expanded feature view with bio + career timeline

pages.layout_pattern: Live tour and broadcast calendar — month-grid calendar with neon-pin event dots, sidebar list of upcoming dates, each event card includes venue, city, doors/start time, supporting shows, ticket CTA, and a 'simulcast on Inner Circle' badge where the live tape will stream to members
sections: Month-grid calendar with neon-pin event dots
Event detail cards (venue, time, supporting shows, tickets)
Photo gallery from past live tapings

pages.layout_pattern: Membership lounge — atmospheric hero with looping concentric ring animation, two-tier pricing comparison (Late Pass / Skeleton Key) with lime accent on the recommended tier, perks lockup grid, gated 'after-hours feed' preview with a blurred episode list, member-only Discord ribbon, and an FAQ accordion
sections: Atmospheric membership hero with concentric ring loop
Two-tier pricing comparison (Late Pass / Skeleton Key)
Perks lockup grid (ad-free, after-hours feed, simulcasts, merch, Discord)
Gated after-hours feed preview (blurred episode list)

pages.layout_pattern: Advertiser pitch page — scrollable narrative with animated audience counters, listener psychographic chart, host-read sample audio clips with play buttons, downloadable media kit PDF, partner logos wall, and a tiered inquiry form (sponsorship / brand partnership / live event underwriting)
sections: Host-read sample clips with play buttons
Tiered inquiry form (sponsorship / brand partnership / live underwriting)

pages.layout_pattern: Network manifesto and team — long-form editorial scroll opening with a typewritten-style mission statement, founding timeline rendered as a vertical waveform with year markers, staff portrait grid (operations, producers, sound engineers), and a careers / pitch-a-show sub-section
sections: Operations and production team portrait grid

message_intent: Convey curatorial prestige and editorial atmosphere
Make the archive feel like a deep, searchable crate
Tier the audience: casual ↔ Late Pass ↔ Skeleton Key
Position the network as a premium ad environment
Recruit new hosts via a credible pitch path
content_model: Channel / Show (channel number, accent color, hosts, cadence, seasons)
Season (number, run dates, episode count, art)
Episode (audio, chapters, transcript, guests, mentioned links, mailbag)
Voice / Host (portrait, bio, timeline, shows, self-selected episodes)
Live broadcast (venue, datetime, supporting shows, simulcast flag)
interactions: Global persistent audio player with concentric-ring waveform and chapter jump
Vertical mini-player rail on desktop / docked bar on mobile
Full-text transcript search across the network with snippet highlighting
Channel-hop strip on the homepage to instantly preview each show
Season tabs and self-selected host episode lists
Gated member feed preview with blur
Calendar pin hover for live broadcast detail
asset_needs: Per-show logotypes and accent color tokens
Partner logos and host-read sample clips
accepted_concept.mobile_behavior: Vertical stack reflow; the desktop right-edge waveform rail collapses into a bottom-docked mini-player; archive filters become a slide-up sheet; calendar view defaults to list with a tap-to-open month grid.

strengths: Eight concrete pages well above the five-page floor, each with a distinct layout pattern and section list
Motif (oscilloscope waveform + glossy chrome panels + magenta→lime gradient washes) is specific and tied to the seed palette
Interactions are domain-specific and useful: docked global player, transcript-synced auto-scroll, hover waveform previews, subscribe-everywhere chip rack
Strong content model coverage (Show / Episode / Host / Tour / Membership / Sponsor) matches the seed brief precisely
Membership tier names (Listener / Insider / Patron) and Hum Club branding feel coherent and label-like
weaknesses: Home and shows-index lean a touch on familiar streaming-service tropes (tile grid + horizontal rail)
Advertise page is a fairly standard B2B scroll without a strongly distinctive motif beat
Hosts page is lighter on metadata depth than the rest of the site

strengths: Ten pages, all with distinct layout patterns — well over the five-page floor and avoids near-duplicate templates
Channel-guide framing (numbered channels, per-show accent hue, liner-notes episode rows) is a fresh and editorially specific take on the seed
Concentric tuner-dial waveform motif plus low-magenta-lit portrait photography is concrete, atmospheric, and on-palette
Cross-network archive page with full-text transcript search, faceted filters and highlighted snippets is a standout, domain-specific feature
Membership tiering (Late Pass / Skeleton Key) and Inner Circle simulcast badge tie the club tier directly into live events
About / pitch-a-show page closes a credible loop for recruiting new hosts, which the seed audience explicitly includes
Vertical right-edge waveform rail on desktop collapsing to a docked mini-player on mobile is a thoughtful, distinctive global-player treatment
weaknesses: Membership and partner pages still follow recognizable conversion-landing patterns, even if dressed in the motif
Channel-guide row list could risk visual monotony if accent hues aren't carefully varied
Some overlap between archive page and per-show episode list — needs disciplined differentiation in execution

issues.message: Form controls on /about (pitch-a-show), /archive (search), /live (venue booking) and /partner (sponsorship inquiry) are missing programmatic labels / accessible names.

Layout consistency scores dip on /inner-circle (48.7) and /live (50.6); the deterministic alignment checker flags row-bottom and horizontal-alignment mismatches, but visually the pages read as intentional editorial composition rather than broken layout.

Inner-circle perks lockup group scored low (26.3) because tile children vary; visually the tiles are consistent. Live event-card group scored 48.5 because event cards intentionally vary (members-only RSVP vs Buy Tickets vs Tickets-on-sale states) which is correct content modelling, not a defect.

repair_instructions: Add explicit <label> elements (or aria-label attributes) tied to every form input on about.html (pitch-a-show form), archive.html (transcript search), live.html (venue booking) and partner.html (sponsorship inquiry) so screen readers announce each field. Visible field labels are already present above each input — wire them up with for/id pairs.

deterministic_checks.page_presence_resolved: All 10 declared concept pages have concrete implementations. The two 'not found' entries (/channels/03-the-late-signal and /channels/03-the-late-signal/s2e07-the-quiet-hour) are route templates that are satisfied by channels/the-late-signal.html and episode.html respectively, both clearly rendered in the screenshots.
